response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend software engineer with a strong track record in web platforms, low-latency services, and developer tooling, but his background has no meaningful overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, and GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His most hardware-adjacent experience is a bachelor's thesis on wave field synthesis and C/C++ work at National Instruments on a measurement IDE, neither of which is relevant to cloud AI hardware co-design at the principal level.
strengths: Strong CS foundation from ETH Zürich with 15+ years of diverse engineering experience
Demonstrated performance-sensitive software work at Sociomantic, including low-latency async I/O in DLang handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq, showing willingness to engage with AI-adjacent domains
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or any chip-level architecture work
No background in GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD design, or deep neural network training and inference optimization
Entire career is concentrated in web, mobile, and backend software engineering — a fundamentally different discipline from cloud hardware and AI accelerator optimization

key_factors.factor: No AI/deep-learning workload or GPU expertise
